mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: Docs: Consider adding validation of checksums or signatures to OPA install documentation
mission.description: Docs: Consider adding validation of checksums or signatures to OPA install documentation. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-policy-agent-opa- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-policy-agent-opa- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
helm list -n open-policy-agent-opa- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-policy-agent-opa- installation.

steps.title: Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-
```
Currently the OPA [installation documentation](https://www.openpolicyagent.org/docs/latest/#running-opa) involves downloading and execution a binary program

At the moment, there's not mention in the documentation of how to validate a digital

steps.title: Apply the fix for Docs: Consider adding validation of checksums or signatures…
steps.description:
Adds sha256 checksum to all binaries

These checksums can be used to verify file integrity.
```yaml
Validate the binary (optional)

Download the kubectl checksum file:

curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl.sha256"
Validate the kubectl binary against the checksum file:

echo "$(<kubectl.sha256) kubectl" | sha256sum --check
If valid, the output is:

kubectl: OK
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Docs: Consider adding validation of checksums or signatures…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds sha256 checksum to all binaries

These checksums can be used to verify file integrity.
codeSnippets:
Validate the binary (optional)

Download the kubectl checksum file:

curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl.sha256"
Validate the kubectl binary against the checksum file:

echo "$(<kubectl.sha256) kubectl" | sha256sum --check
If valid, the output is:

kubectl: OK

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-policy-agent-opa- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
